statement: Clearly Canadian was founded in 1987 in British Columbia, Canada.
The parent company is The Clearly Food & Beverage Company ULC in Canada.
The US entity is The Clearly Food & Beverage Company LLC, based in Toronto.
Clearly Canadian's headquarters are in Toronto, Ontario.
Paul Tepperman is the CEO of Clearly Canadian.
Jennifer Blackburn serves as VP Marketing and Interim CMO at Clearly Canadian; she is a Laurier Class of 2001 alumna.
Clearly Canadian's mission is to be a leading lifestyle brand that promotes good thoughts, good words, and good deeds.
Clearly Canadian's tagline is 'Clearly it's our Water.'
Clearly Canadian was one of the first 'new age beverages' ever created, helping launch a multibillion-dollar category.
Since 1987, Clearly Canadian has used real sugar (not HFCS), natural flavours, and no food colourings — before clean-label was mainstream.
Clearly Canadian is described as 'Born in British Columbia, raised by a generation of 80s and 90s kids.'
Clearly Canadian is described as 'the OG of premium-flavoured sparkling water' and 'North America's premium sparkling alternative.'
Clearly Canadian was acquired in 2012 by 4NCapital Partners.
Clearly Canadian was sold in 2021 to private investors.
Clearly Canadian filed for bankruptcy in 2010, with production resuming after a successful crowdfunding campaign.
Clearly Canadian relaunched in retail in 2017 after its crowdfunding-fuelled comeback.
Clearly Canadian's crowdfunding campaign attracted 14,000+ fans across 35 countries, pre-selling 40,000+ cases in every US state and Canadian province.
Rhett and Link of Good Mythical Morning promoted Clearly Canadian's crowdfunding campaign.
source: Good Mythical Morning / Clearly Canadian campaign records

statement: Clearly Canadian has shipped 50M+ bottles since its 2017 retail relaunch.
Clearly Canadian appeared on Seinfeld, Friends, and Sex and the City during its 1990s peak.
source: Pop culture records / Clearly Canadian brand history

statement: Clearly Canadian's peak 1990s annual sales approached $200M.
Clearly Canadian experienced rapid growth in the 1990s alongside Snapple and Red Bull, defining the 'new age beverage' era.
Clearly Canadian runs the Clearly Community Challenge, a give-back program supporting crowdfunding campaigns and community causes.
Clearly Canadian is positioned as a 'unicorn' brand straddling the sparkling water and soda categories — real flavour without the guilt.
Clearly Canadian's primary target consumer is soda drinkers seeking a better-for-you alternative, not sparkling water drinkers.
Approximately 80% of Clearly Canadian's volume comes from soda drinkers seeking better-for-you options.
Clearly Canadian VP stated: 'The guilt and shame soda consumers have around drinking sodas is really interesting' — positioning CC as the guilt-free bridge.
Clearly Canadian VP: 'Being very strategic and true to the brand — we want to build it the right way.'
Clearly Canadian VP: 'We never want to be in an instance where this brand goes away again.'
All Clearly Canadian Originals are made with sparkling Canadian spring water as the base.
Clearly Canadian Originals use natural fruit flavours — no artificial colours or flavourings.
Clearly Canadian Originals have never used high-fructose corn syrup (HFCS) — sweetened with real sugar since 1987.
source: Clearly Canadian product labeling / company history

statement: Clearly Canadian Originals contain 90 calories per 12oz/355mL serving.
Clearly Canadian Originals contain 24g of sugar per 12oz/355mL serving.
The Originals lineup is the mid-calorie full-flavoured sparkling spring water category leader.
Clearly Canadian Originals represent approximately 85% of the brand's total portfolio mix.
source: Clearly Canadian internal data via BevNET 2024

statement: Mountain Blackberry is a flagship Clearly Canadian Original: 90 cal, 24g sugar per 12oz, available in glass bottle and SleekCan 6-pack.
Wild Cherry is a Clearly Canadian Original: 90 cal, 24g sugar per 12oz, available in glass bottle and SleekCan 6-pack.
Orchard Peach is a Clearly Canadian Original: 90 cal, 24g sugar per 12oz, available in glass bottle and SleekCan 6-pack.
Summer Strawberry was brought back at the end of 2022 and is historically Clearly Canadian's #1 flavour.
Summer Strawberry Original: 90 cal, 24g sugar per 12oz, available in glass bottle and SleekCan 6-pack.
Clearly Canadian sources spring water from Whistler, British Columbia.
source: Clearly Canadian product labeling / company materials

statement: Clearly Canadian sources spring water from Formosa, Ontario.
source: Clearly Canadian product labeling / company materials

statement: All Clearly Canadian spring water is sourced from Canadian springs — a core part of the brand's identity.
Clearly Canadian Zero Sugar launched in July 2023, primarily in the US with Canadian expansion underway.
Clearly Canadian Zero Sugar contains 0 calories and 0 sugar per serving.
Clearly Canadian Zero Sugar is sweetened with stevia — no artificial sweeteners.
Forest Blackberry is a Clearly Canadian Zero Sugar flavour: 0 cal, 0 sugar, stevia sweetened.
Fresh Cherry is a Clearly Canadian Zero Sugar flavour: 0 cal, 0 sugar, stevia sweetened.
Citrus Medley is a Clearly Canadian Zero Sugar flavour: 0 cal, 0 sugar, stevia sweetened.
Tropical Splash is a Clearly Canadian Zero Sugar flavour: 0 cal, 0 sugar, stevia sweetened.
Clearly Canadian Essence Grapefruit: 0 cal, 0 sugar, no sweeteners of any kind — only spring water and natural vegan flavours.
Clearly Canadian Essence Limon: 0 cal, 0 sugar, no sweeteners — only spring water and natural vegan flavours.
Clearly Canadian Essence won InnoBev 'Best in Glass' award.
Clearly Canadian Sparkling Mineral Water is plain sparkling water from a Canadian spring — no flavours added.
Clearly Canadian Still Mineral Water is a non-carbonated spring water that has been featured on QVC.
CC Maple is a proposed new product featuring natural Canadian maple flavour.
CC Maple is designed as both a standalone sparkling drink and a cocktail/mocktail mixer.
CC Maple is planned in both glass bottle format for bar/on-premise and SleekCan for retail.
CC Maple pairs naturally with bourbon, whiskey, and gin as a premium cocktail mixer.
CC Maple is planned to launch first in bar/on-premise channels, then expand to retail.
CC Maple targets cocktail culture enthusiasts and the sober-curious movement as key consumer segments.
The Clearly Canadian glass bottle is the brand's most recognizable asset — its distinctive bulb shape is iconic.
The glass bottle's bulb shape was designed to feel like a baseball in the hand — the founder was a baseball fan.
Clearly Canadian SleekCans are 12oz/355mL and available in Originals and Zero Sugar lines.
Clearly Canadian SleekCan 6-packs are priced at approximately $7.99, roughly 50% less than the glass bottle equivalent.
Single Clearly Canadian glass bottles retail for approximately $2.49–$2.99.
Single Clearly Canadian cans retail for approximately $1.49–$1.99.
Clearly Canadian uses only glass and aluminum packaging — never plastic.
Clearly Canadian packaging uses 100% recycled materials.
'Eco by design' is a core brand pillar for Clearly Canadian.
Clearly Canadian SleekCans are described as 'CC's biggest innovation launch since retail reintroduction in 2017.'
Clearly Canadian Essence won InnoBev Awards 2024 'Best in Glass.'
Clearly Canadian Sparkling Mineral Water was an InnoBev 2024 'Best in Glass' finalist.
Clearly Canadian Original Cans were an InnoBev 2024 'Best in Can' finalist.
Clearly Canadian Essence was an InnoBev 2024 'Best Premium Drink' finalist.
The Shania Twain campaign (June 2025) was Clearly Canadian's first advertising campaign since 1991 — a 34-year gap.
Shania Twain — with 100M+ records sold, multiple Grammys, from Timmins, Ontario — stars in Clearly Canadian's 2025 campaign.
Clearly Canadian's 2025 campaign was created by Ryan Reynolds' Maximum Effort production company.
The Shania Twain campaign was filmed on location in Canada, featuring natural waterfalls and forest backdrops.
In the campaign, Shania Twain plays a treasure hunter in a high-energy chase through the Canadian wilderness.
The Shania Twain campaign includes a catchy original jingle.
The campaign tagline references 'Clearly Canadian is back' and plays on Shania Twain's 'Still the One.'
Shania Twain described Clearly Canadian as 'pure nostalgia in a bottle.'
Ryan Reynolds commented on Instagram: 'This fills my Vancouver heart to the brim' about the Clearly Canadian campaign.
source: Ryan Reynolds Instagram / Strategy Magazine July 2025

statement: CMO Blackburn stated the campaign targets 'new nostalgics' aged 30–50.
CMO Blackburn described the Shania Twain campaign as 'the largest marketing investment since relaunch.'
CMO Blackburn: 'Strategic evolution — investing with purpose and discipline.'
CMO Blackburn: 'More to come — new innovations, seasonal creative, partnerships.'
Agencies on the Shania Twain campaign include Maximum Effort, Buzzphoria, Jan Kelley, Lede Company, and Yellow House Consulting.
The Shania Twain campaign debuted at the SFA Summer Fancy Food Show in June 2025.
Clearly Canadian exhibited at Expo West 2025.
Clearly Canadian runs 'Tag a Retailer' social campaigns asking fans to identify which stores should carry CC.
Clearly Canadian US multi-outlet retail dollar sales reached $65.8M in the 52 weeks ending July 2024 (Circana data).
Clearly Canadian achieved 39.4% year-over-year US retail growth in the 52 weeks ending July 2024 (Circana).
Total year-over-year growth across all revenue streams was approximately 55%, per Clearly Canadian VP.
Clearly Canadian describes itself as 'growing at a market-leading pace.'
Clearly Canadian is available in approximately 33,600 retail doors across North America.
source: Clearly Canadian internal data via BevNET 2024

statement: Clearly Canadian has approximately 69% ACV in conventional grocery in North America.
source: Clearly Canadian internal data via BevNET 2024

statement: Clearly Canadian is available at Loblaws in Canada.
Clearly Canadian is available at Sobeys in Canada.
Clearly Canadian is available at Shoppers Drug Mart in Canada.
Clearly Canadian is available at Farm Boy in Canada.
Clearly Canadian is available at FreshCo in Canada.
Clearly Canadian is available at Metro Ontario in Canada.
Clearly Canadian is available at Circle K convenience stores in Canada.
Clearly Canadian is available at 7-Eleven in Canada.
Clearly Canadian is available at London Drug in Canada.
Clearly Canadian is available at Whole Foods Canada.
Clearly Canadian is available on Amazon Canada for direct-to-consumer purchase.
Clearly Canadian is available at all Kroger banners in the US.
Clearly Canadian is available at Ahold-Delhaize banners in the US.
Clearly Canadian is available at Albertsons and Safeway in the US.
Clearly Canadian is available at Publix in the US.
Clearly Canadian is available at H-E-B in the US.
Clearly Canadian is available at Meijer in the US.
Clearly Canadian is available at Food Lion in the US.
Clearly Canadian is available at Acme Markets in the US.
Clearly Canadian is available on Amazon US for direct-to-consumer purchase.
Clearly Canadian is actively expanding into club, mass, and convenience channels in the US.
Clearly Canadian appeared on QVC in February–March 2025 for direct-to-consumer TV retail.
Clearly Canadian became the official sparkling water of the Toronto Blue Jays under a multi-year deal announced March 2025.
source: Clearly Canadian / Toronto Blue Jays press release March 2025

statement: Blue Jays fans at Rogers Centre were the first in Canada to access Clearly Canadian SleekCans.
source: Clearly Canadian / Toronto Blue Jays press release March 2025

statement: Clearly Canadian began appearing at Rogers Centre (Blue Jays stadium) starting March 27, 2025.
source: Clearly Canadian / Toronto Blue Jays press release March 2025

statement: Clearly Canadian flavours available at Rogers Centre include Mountain Blackberry, Wild Cherry, and Sparkling Mineral Water.
source: Clearly Canadian / Toronto Blue Jays partnership details

statement: Clearly Canadian has signage at all Rogers Centre events as part of the Blue Jays partnership.
source: Clearly Canadian / Toronto Blue Jays partnership details

statement: Clearly Canadian CEO: 'Baseball inspired the design of our iconic blue glass bottles.'
source: Clearly Canadian / Toronto Blue Jays press release March 2025

statement: vs. LaCroix/Bubly: Clearly Canadian offers real flavour, spring water from source, heritage glass bottle, and authentic brand heritage — not just unflavoured sparkling water.
source: Clearly Canadian brand materials / BevNET analysis 2024

statement: vs. Poppi/Olipop: Clearly Canadian is sparkling spring water (not prebiotic soda), lower calorie in Zero/Essence lines, and carries genuine brand heritage since 1987.
vs. Liquid Death: Clearly Canadian offers real fruit flavour, sweetness options, glass packaging alongside aluminum, and strong Canadian identity.
vs. Topo Chico: Clearly Canadian is Canadian-origin, offers more flavour variety, and carries a compelling comeback story.
Clearly Canadian occupies a 'unicorn' position straddling sparkling water and soda — real flavour without soda's guilt.
The iconic glass bottle is a key competitive differentiator that no major sparkling water competitor replicates at scale.
Clearly Canadian's 1987 heritage gives it authentic nostalgia credentials that newer sparkling water brands cannot replicate.
Clearly Canadian's Canadian origin and spring water sourcing are authentic differentiators in a market of brands that claim premium positioning without Canadian roots.
Nostalgia loyalists — consumers who remember Clearly Canadian from the 1980s–90s — are a core and highly engaged consumer segment.
Gen Z consumers are discovering Clearly Canadian via nostalgia content on TikTok and Instagram, treating it as a culturally cool heritage brand.
Millennials who grew up with Clearly Canadian in the 90s represent a strong nostalgia-driven repurchase segment.
The sober-curious segment is a growing Clearly Canadian consumer group, especially for Zero Sugar and CC Maple as mocktail mixers.
Health and wellness consumers are drawn to Clearly Canadian for its clean label: spring water, natural flavours, no HFCS, no artificial colours.
Bartenders and mixologists are an emerging target for Clearly Canadian, particularly for CC Maple as a premium cocktail mixer.
Household decision makers — often Millennial or Gen X parents — drive Clearly Canadian multipacks as a family better-for-you soda replacement.
Convenience-first shoppers who discover Clearly Canadian in c-stores are an important incremental volume driver.
Premium lifestyle consumers drawn to glass bottle aesthetics and natural Canadian spring water represent a high-value, high-engagement segment.
The US market is Clearly Canadian's strongest growth engine, driven by Zero Sugar and Originals expansion.
Canada is Clearly Canadian's home market with deep emotional resonance — the brand is seen as a national heritage product.
The largest consumer opportunity is soda switchers — people who want flavour and sweetness but are reducing or eliminating soft drinks.
The $7.99 SleekCan 6-pack addresses price-sensitive consumers who find glass bottle single-serves prohibitive at $2.49–$2.99 each.
On-premise (restaurants, bars, stadiums) is an under-penetrated channel for Clearly Canadian with significant growth potential, especially with CC Maple.
Ecommerce via Amazon Canada and Amazon US is a growing channel enabling nationwide direct-to-consumer reach.
The Zero Sugar line was launched to capture the zero-calorie beverage trend while maintaining Clearly Canadian's natural-ingredients philosophy.
Clearly Canadian Essence fills the pure-sparkling-water niche with zero calories and zero sweeteners — competing with Perrier and S. Pellegrino on naturalness while beating them on flavour.
CC Maple leverages Canada's most iconic flavour — maple — to deepen the brand's Canadian identity and expand into premium cocktail occasions.
Clearly Canadian's commitment to glass and aluminum only (never plastic) is central to its sustainability and brand identity.
Sourcing from named Canadian springs (Whistler BC, Formosa ON) demonstrates traceable, responsible water sourcing.
Clearly Canadian uses 100% recycled materials in packaging — glass and aluminum are infinitely recyclable.
Clearly Canadian Essence uses only natural vegan flavours — no animal-derived ingredients of any kind.
Clearly Canadian's nostalgia positioning is authentic — the brand genuinely disappeared and came back, unlike manufactured retro branding.
Clearly Canadian Originals have a simple, recognizable ingredient list: sparkling Canadian spring water, cane sugar, natural flavours.
Clearly Canadian proudly markets its Canadian spring water origin as a premium quality signal unavailable from US-sourced competitors.
When Clearly Canadian relaunched in retail in 2017, it was one of the first major nostalgia beverage comebacks in the industry.
Clearly Canadian's brand voice is optimistic, nostalgic, and playfully Canadian — never preachy or clinical.
The 'good thoughts, good words, good deeds' mission informs CC's tone: positive, community-oriented, and values-led.
Clearly Canadian's price architecture spans three tiers: Essence/Sparkling (plain water premium), Originals Glass (heritage premium), and Cans/6-packs (accessible value).
Clearly Canadian expands flavours strategically — Summer Strawberry's return in 2022 proved demand for flavour nostalgia before launching Zero Sugar in 2023.
Clearly Canadian's crowdfunding campaign pre-sold 40,000+ cases reaching every US state and Canadian province — demonstrating pan-North American demand before retail relaunch.
At InnoBev Awards 2024, Clearly Canadian won Best in Glass (Essence) and was finalist in Best in Glass (Sparkling), Best in Can (Originals), and Best Premium Drink (Essence).
Clearly Canadian's current portfolio spans: Originals (glass + can), Zero Sugar (can), Essence (glass), Sparkling Mineral Water (glass), Still Mineral Water, and CC Maple (proposed).
Clearly Canadian Zero Sugar launched primarily in the US in July 2023 and is actively expanding into Canadian retail.
The SleekCan launch opened Clearly Canadian to new shoppers in the can-format sparkling water aisle who would not purchase a glass bottle.
The Blue Jays partnership aligns two iconic Canadian brands — Clearly Canadian and Canada's only MLB team — around shared national pride.
source: Clearly Canadian / Toronto Blue Jays press release March 2025

statement: Shania Twain's Ontario roots and Canadian identity make her an authentic match for Clearly Canadian's BC-born, nationally proud brand.
All four core Clearly Canadian Original flavours — Mountain Blackberry, Wild Cherry, Orchard Peach, Summer Strawberry — are available in the iconic glass bottle.
All four core Clearly Canadian Original flavours are available in SleekCan 6-packs.
'New nostalgics' — the 30-50 age group targeted by the Shania campaign — are defined by emotional connection to the 90s brand and willingness to pay premium for authentic heritage.
In 38+ years, Clearly Canadian has never used artificial colours, artificial flavours, or HFCS — a consistent clean-label record.
source: Clearly Canadian company history / product labeling

statement: Clearly Canadian operates simultaneously in both Canada and the US — a true cross-border North American brand.
Clearly Canadian Originals (90 cal) fill the 'mid-calorie' gap between zero-calorie sparkling waters and full-calorie sodas — a category largely abandoned by big soda brands.
Bar and cocktail occasions represent a new use case for Clearly Canadian — particularly CC Maple — bringing the brand into premium on-premise drinking culture.
The glass bottle's premium aesthetic makes Clearly Canadian a genuine gifting option — particularly in gift baskets and Canadiana-themed sets.
With ~85% of portfolio volume, Clearly Canadian Originals drive the vast majority of revenue and brand exposure.
source: Clearly Canadian internal data via BevNET 2024

statement: CMO Blackburn signalled seasonal creative executions and new innovations are planned beyond the Shania Twain campaign.
Clearly Canadian's bankruptcy-to-comeback story is itself a brand asset — a narrative of consumer love powerful enough to resurrect a brand.
The distinctive cobalt-blue colour of Clearly Canadian's glass bottle is an instantly recognizable shelf presence.
Clearly Canadian's 'good deeds' pillar is operationalized through the Clearly Community Challenge, supporting real consumer-led causes.
Clearly Canadian primarily competes on shelf in the sparkling water aisle, not the soda aisle — a strategic placement that signals better-for-you.
By choosing stevia over sucralose or aspartame for Zero Sugar, Clearly Canadian maintains its natural-ingredients promise even in the diet line.
source: Clearly Canadian product labeling / brand philosophy

statement: Crowdfunding demand came from 35 countries — proving Clearly Canadian has latent global brand recognition beyond North America.
QVC appearances in Feb-March 2025 targeted older, more affluent Millennial and Gen X households with bundled Clearly Canadian packages.
CC Maple positions as a sophisticated non-alcoholic option for sober-curious consumers at bars — a mocktail that doesn't feel like a compromise.
Shania Twain serves as Clearly Canadian's celebrity spokesperson for the 2025 campaign — the first formal celebrity partnership since relaunch.
The SleekCan launch is a significant contributor to Clearly Canadian's 55% total revenue growth by unlocking new retail shelf space and new price points.
Clearly Canadian is positioned as 'North America's premium sparkling alternative' — a claim backed by Canadian spring water, glass packaging, and 38-year clean-label heritage.
For health-conscious consumers, Clearly Canadian Originals function as a treat — real flavour and sweetness without the volume of calories from full soda.
The glass bottle's tactile, weighted feel differentiates the drinking experience from plastic or thin-walled aluminum — contributing to perceived premium quality.
source: Clearly Canadian brand materials / consumer research

statement: Unlike brands that use artificial retro aesthetics, Clearly Canadian's nostalgia is grounded in a real history — it genuinely was a 1990s cultural icon.
Clearly Canadian's stated mission is to be a 'leading lifestyle brand' — not just a beverage — encompassing values, community, and identity.
Clearly Canadian's 'never plastic' commitment is a standing brand promise — not a campaign — applying to every product in every market.
Clearly Canadian's US sales data ($65.8M, +39.4% YoY) is verified by Circana multi-outlet retail tracking — the beverage industry standard measurement source.
The SleekCan format skews younger than the glass bottle, attracting Gen Z and younger Millennials who shop the can sparkling water set.
Maximum Effort's track record of high-impact, culturally resonant brand campaigns (Aviation Gin, Mint Mobile) aligns with Clearly Canadian's ambition to re-enter culture — not just retail.
